description:
lib.rs:171 constructs reqwest::Client::new() with no timeout. reqwest::Client default timeout is None — a Vault server that accepts the connection but never responds will leave the future pending forever.

Trigger: any network condition that holds the connection open without progress (firewall blackhole, sealed Vault that hangs, half-broken proxy).

Bad behavior: callers waiting on get()/put() never see Unavailable; a daemon's secret-fetch task hangs and never retries. SecretError::Unavailable cannot be returned because the future never completes.

Fix: reqwest::Client::builder().timeout(Duration::from_secs(N)).build() with a sensible default (e.g. 30s) and/or expose ?timeout= in the URI. Same pattern applies to peer crates secretx-doppler and secretx-gcp-sm; consider a shared helper in secretx-core but file the per-crate fix here.
title: No HTTP timeout — hung Vault server blocks get/put indefinitely
